purpose: Additional long-form video candidates based on documented talks, letters, conversations, and witness accounts from Orthodox elders, Optina monks, Mount Athos elders, and Russian monastic saints.
editorial_rules: Use letters and official saint lives as factual backbone.
Use modern copyrighted biographies only for verification and paraphrase; do not quote long passages.
Label miracle accounts and oral testimonies as witness accounts or tradition, not as modern laboratory proof.
Avoid speculative prophecy content unless it is directly relevant and sourced responsibly.
Prefer stories with a clear dramatic arc: visitor arrives with pain, elder responds, life changes.

source_library.title: Venerable Ambrose of Optina
source_library.usable_for: Life structure, daily routine, Dostoevsky visit, elder receiving visitors, letter ministry.
source_library.rights_note: Use as citation and paraphrased summary.

source_library.title: Letters of Guidance by Elder Ambrose of Optina
source_library.usable_for: Documented spiritual counsel, letter-based episodes, advice on judging, humility, illness, grief.
source_library.rights_note: Quote sparingly; paraphrase counsel into narration.

source_library.title: Optina's Elders: Instructors of Monks and Conversers with Angels
source_library.usable_for: Optina as a place-centered story, Ambrose and Nektary context, Russian spiritual hunger.
source_library.rights_note: Use as research and attribution; paraphrase.

source_library.title: Optina Elders
source_library.usable_for: Optina elders overview, elder Ambrose receiving rich and poor, learned and unlettered.

source_library.title: Venerable Paisios of the Holy Mountain
source_library.usable_for: Paisios life chronology, Cappadocia childhood, Mount Athos return, asceticism.
source_library.rights_note: Use as factual backbone and citation.

source_library.title: St. Paisios: Let our soul always thirst for God
source_library.usable_for: Conversation-based video structure about longing for God, modern anxiety, and spiritual thirst.
source_library.rights_note: Modern translation/source; paraphrase and cite. Do not reproduce long transcript.

source_library.title: Saint Paisios and the Greek Hindus
source_library.usable_for: Witness-account episode about a long conversation with Paisios and spiritual discernment.
source_library.rights_note: Use carefully as testimony; avoid sensationalism.

source_library.title: Saint Silouan the Athonite by Archimandrite Sophrony
source_library.type: book record / primary modern biography and writings collection
source_library.usable_for: Canonical source to verify Silouan's life and teachings through Sophrony.
source_library.rights_note: Copyrighted modern book; use only for verification and short attributed references.

source_library.title: Saint Silouan the Athonite
source_library.usable_for: Life summary, Athonite context, repose date.

source_library.title: Venerable Paisios Velichkovsky
source_library.usable_for: Russian/Athonite monastic renewal, elder guidance, translation of patristic texts.
source_library.rights_note: Use as factual backbone for a Russian monastic renewal episode.

video_candidates.title: The Elder Who Answered Two Hundred Souls a Day
video_candidates.format: documentary story built from official life plus letters
video_candidates.hook: In a small Russian monastery, sick and exhausted, one monk became a spiritual hospital for an entire nation.
story_beats: Open with Ambrose's cell already surrounded by visitors and letters.
Flash back to his illness and promise to become a monk.
Show Optina as Russia's spiritual refuge.
Explain what eldership meant: not prediction, but spiritual diagnosis.
Use letters as documented counsel scenes: grief, judging others, illness, humility.
Bring in Dostoevsky's visit after the death of Alyosha and the later echo in Starets Zosima.
End with Ambrose as a man physically weak but spiritually inexhaustible.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: Changed lives after conversations with the elder.
Dostoevsky's encounter as a documented cultural witness.
Ambrose receiving rich, poor, educated, and simple people with tailored counsel.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Optina monastery exteriors, candlelit cell, stacks of letters, Russian winter roads, Dostoevsky-era interiors, handwritten counsel overlays.
video_candidates.rights_risk: Low to medium. Avoid long direct letter quotations.

video_candidates.title: The Monk Who Told the Modern World to Thirst for God
video_candidates.hook: People climbed the mountain looking for answers, but Paisios kept turning them toward one question: what does your soul thirst for?
story_beats: Open with a pilgrim sitting near an elder's hut on Mount Athos.
Ground Paisios in Cappadocia, refugee childhood, and Athonite life.
Frame the taped conversation as a documented spiritual encounter.
Explore spiritual thirst versus distraction, comfort, and modern anxiety.
Show why his counsel felt simple but cut deeply.
End with the image of an elder hidden in poverty becoming a guide for a restless century.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: Visitor testimonies of discernment and consolation.
Accounts of hours-long conversations with pilgrims.
Use miracle claims only as attributed witness accounts.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Mount Athos paths, a humble cell, prayer rope, rough wooden bench, dawn over the sea, handwritten counsel cards.
video_candidates.rights_risk: Medium. Modern conversations must be paraphrased and cited.

video_candidates.title: The Athonite Elder and the Men Searching in the Wrong Place
video_candidates.hook: They came to Mount Athos carrying spiritual confusion from the modern world. Paisios did not argue with them. He listened until the night became prayer.
story_beats: Open with the midnight setting and a conversation continuing toward the monastery service.
Explain why Mount Athos attracted seekers, not only believers.
Introduce the testimony as a witness account, not a courtroom transcript.
Show Paisios' method: patience, discernment, clarity without cruelty.
Contrast spiritual curiosity with repentance and commitment.
End with the idea that the elder's real miracle was seeing the human soul without contempt.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: Witness account of a long conversation with Paisios.
Possible life change after the meeting, presented as testimony.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Night at Athos, monastery bells, dim cell interior, pilgrims walking with lanterns, Midnight Office atmosphere.
video_candidates.rights_risk: Medium. Avoid copying testimony wording; identify as personal testimony.

video_candidates.title: Keep Your Mind in Hell: The Monk Who Survived Despair
video_candidates.hook: One sentence saved a monk from despair, and it became one of the most severe teachings in modern Christian spirituality.
story_beats: Open with Silouan's interior darkness on Athos.
Trace his Russian village childhood and arrival at Saint Panteleimon Monastery.
Explain the famous counsel carefully, without turning it into self-hatred.
Explore humility, prayer for enemies, and spiritual warfare.
Use Sophrony as the documented transmitter of Silouan's writings.
End with why Silouan speaks powerfully to people trapped between pride and despair.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: The inner encounter/counsel is treated as hagiographic spiritual testimony.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Russian village, Athonite monastery, night prayer, handwritten phrase reveal, candles and sea wind.
video_candidates.rights_risk: Medium-high. Sophrony source is copyrighted; paraphrase and keep direct quotes minimal.

video_candidates.title: The Monastery That Became Russia's Spiritual Hospital
video_candidates.hook: Before therapy offices and self-help books, Russia had a monastery where writers, peasants, nobles, and broken souls came for one thing: a word from an elder.
story_beats: Open with a road to Optina and people arriving from every class.
Explain eldership and why it mattered in Russian spiritual life.
Introduce Leonid, Macarius, Ambrose, and later Nektary as a chain of counsel.
Show Optina's link to literature and the Russian intelligentsia.
Build scenes around confession, counsel, letters, and silence.
End with Optina as a hospital of souls rather than a museum of saints.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: Visitors whose lives changed after conversations.
Optina's reputation as a place of prophetic counsel, framed carefully.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Monastery exterior, forest road, pilgrims, writing desk, chapel candles, Russian literary portraits as context.

video_candidates.title: The Athonite Translator Who Rebuilt Monastic Prayer
video_candidates.hook: A monk went to Athos looking for a teacher. He returned with a library that helped revive the prayer of the heart across the Slavic world.
story_beats: Open with manuscripts being copied by candlelight.
Show Paisios seeking elder guidance and refusing shallow monastic life.
Follow him to Mount Athos for patristic copying and translation.
Explain why translation became spiritual warfare against forgetfulness.
Connect his work to later monastic renewal and Optina's spiritual culture.
End with books as living conversations between old monks and future generations.
miracle_or_witness_accounts: Not miracle-driven; focus on providential preservation of prayer tradition.
video_candidates.visual_direction: Manuscripts, Athonite skete, Slavonic text, candlelit copying, monastic library.

next_production_step: Turn SAINTS-013 into a full 1300-1800 word script and scene table after Corporate Shadows first-video analytics are checked.
